The runtime observes the event and mints a receipt. The receipt is mirrored into the Omnibinary ledger as an indexed append-only record with O(1) lookup, preserving provenance. When a candidate is promoted or archived, the Arc-RAR bundle packages the receipts, artifacts, and manifest together so the full lineage is restorable. Rollback is possible because the prior ledger state and prior bundle both remain addressable. Every transition produces evidence; no state change happens without a trace. The boundary between live and archived is explicit.

# ARC Language Module — Architecture (v0.24.0)

## Core separation of concerns

| Concept | Separated from | Why it matters |
|---------|---------------|----------------|
| Language | Script | A language can be written in multiple scripts (Serbian: Cyrl+Latn). Script ≠ language. |

| Genealogy | Software identifier | Glottolog genealogy ≠ ISO 639-3 code ≠ BCP 47 tag. All three are tracked but kept separate. |
| Translation equivalence | Etymological relatedness | Two words can be translations without being cognates; cognates can be false friends. |

Flag the contradiction and preserve both. The fact is two sources disagree; the inference about which is correct is unknown without more evidence. The language module must not silently resolve the conflict — that would destroy the provenance trail and threaten the trust boundary. Only if one source has strictly higher trust provenance and the other is explicitly marked lower-confidence is an automatic resolution conditionally acceptable. Otherwise both records persist, both are flagged, and arbitration is required. The module is the source of truth for its own internal contradictions.
